title: Council call: give us tools to tackle threat of floods
full_text:
The federal government is being urged to help councils build more resilient infrastructure in flood-prone areas and provide access to technology designed to deliver cheaper and more accessible insurance.
With many households being priced out of coverage due to rising flood risks, Geoscape Australia – a data service provider jointly owned by the federal, state and territory governments – says its technology could help reduce insurance costs in low-lying areas. It has developed so-called “first-floor elevation” (FFE) technology, which uses artificial intelligence alongside aerial and ground-level imagery to calculate how susceptible a building is to flooding.
While some insurers impose blanket restrictions on properties located in flood-risk zones, the modelling enables them to gauge exposure more accurately, thus helping insurers to price their premiums more accurately.
Geoscape says the technology could also assist local councils in infrastructure planning and disaster resilience, and is pushing for an announcement in the budget to expand access through the national buildings dataset.
The move echoes calls from the Australian Local Government Association, the peak body representing the nation’s local councils, which has advocated for greater access to high-quality data to strengthen climate resilience.
Geoscape chief executive Dean Capobianco said broader access to the FFE technology would help address one of the biggest “data gaps” facing local governments in planning and development. “Whether it’s disaster recovery, whether it’s resilience planning, whether it’s planning for new housing developments and infrastructure development, having this content and information is really important,” Mr Capobianco said.
summary: Councils are calling for federal support to enhance flood resilience through improved infrastructure and access to technology that could lower insurance costs in flood-prone areas.

heading: General Insurance
key_points: Geoscape Australia has developed 'first-floor elevation' technology to assess flood risk more accurately.
The technology aims to help insurers price premiums more effectively and assist councils in disaster resilience planning.
The Australian Local Government Association is advocating for better access to data for climate resilience.
relevance: This is relevant to actuaries and financial services professionals as it highlights the intersection of risk assessment, insurance pricing, and climate-related financial planning.
